buggy_code:
df['returns'] = df['price'].pct_change()
df['signal'] = df['returns'].shift(1) > 0
df['strategy'] = df['signal'] * df['returns']
clean_code:
df['returns'] = df['price'].pct_change()
df['signal'] = df['returns'].shift(1) > 0
df['strategy'] = df['signal'] * df['returns']
description: Clean example — no bugs. Signal correctly uses past returns via shift(1).
explanation: shift(1) moves data forward in time. The signal at time T uses the return from time T-1. This is causally correct — at time T, you only know returns up to T-1, so you use that information to make a decision for time T. No lookahead bias.
